contents.title: Model-Based Systems Engineering for LLM Pipelines in KOI
contents.content:
# Model-Based Systems Engineering for LLM Pipelines in KOI

**Date**: May 18, 2026
**Lead Author**: @rororowyourboat (Rohan Mehta)
**Contributors**: @ibenmeir (Ilan Ben-Meir), @jshorish (Jamsheed), @sayertindall (Sayer Tindall)
**Domain**: Projects / Scientific Computing

## Problem Statement
When querying the internal BlockScience corpus via `koi-chat`, standard RAG pipelines struggle to preserve the semantic density of our multi-scale modeling research. The KOI protocol helps, but the LLM retrieval systems need an ontology that understands the difference between a cadCAD partial state update and an ethnographic field note.

## System Workflow Design
I am proposing an LLM-augmented workflow based on the research presented at EthCC Paris.
1. **Parsing Module**: Extracts text.
2. **Qualitative Verification**: Working with **Ilan Ben-Meir**, we've crafted an evaluation prompt suite to ensure the literary and narrative context of research (like Kelsie's DAO pieces) is maintained.
3. **Economic Constraints**: **Jamsheed** provided constraints based on computational economics, ensuring our token pricing models for API queries are sustainable under network load.

## Implementation details
This will integrate directly with Sayer's protocol. The LLM will route queries with tags `[Modeling & Simulation]` directly to cadCAD python file schemas (see `bsci-koi-bridge-demo`).

* **Discussion**: [Slack #ai-workflows](https://blockscience.slack.com/archives/CAI889012/p1716039912)
